system_description.overview: BootLoop is an AI agent platform for writing and testing firmware on real embedded hardware. The agent ingests datasheets, schematics, and other design files to generate firmware code and automatically validates it using test equipment such as oscilloscopes, hardware debuggers, simulation frameworks, and the target hardware itself.

products.AI Agent for Embedded Software: Rigorous AI-driven firmware code generation from hardware descriptions (schematics, datasheets, system diagrams, design notes) with automated debugging and validation on real hardware and test equipment

infrastructure.cloud_provider: Microsoft Azure (carve-out — Azure is a subservice organization)
regions: Not explicitly named; single region implied
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (Azure high availability data centers with multiple AZs)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with network ACLs; production environment isolated
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls on application gateway and production network; firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management; Azure security groups and network ACLs
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS/IPS via Azure (intrusion detection and prevention tools configured)
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host with VPN for administrative/management access
network_architecture.tls: TLS 1.3 for all client HTTPS requests
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; email scanning for malicious scripts
network_architecture.topology: Client laptop → TLS 1.3 → Azure API Gateway → VPC (Telemetry service → Azure managed services → database); WorkOS SSO + JWT Auth for authentication; Anthropic (external LLM API) called over TLS 1.3; Azure Key Vault for key management. Management access via bastion host VPN.

compute: Azure managed containers/services (specific compute type not named)
application_architecture.environments: Separate production and staging environments; development/test environments separate from production

databases: Not explicitly named — Azure managed database (type not disclosed)
data_storage.backup_frequency: Regular (formal backup policy; specific frequency not quantified beyond 'suitable backups taken and maintained')

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: WorkOS (SSO provider); Azure (Active Directory, server and database accounts; Azure password policy enforced)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all user accounts with administrative access to production platform; SCM access requires MFA; 2FA required for email access
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Azure password policy: periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements; lockout after failed login attempts; biometrics supported for desktop unlock

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (Azure Key Vault)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: SCM (name not disclosed; MFA enforced; source code changes logged, timestamped, attributed)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Merge to production requires additional approval; branch protection rules configured; developers cannot push directly to production
ci_cd_devops.iac: Baseline configurations retained as code; configuration management tool for implementation and rollback
ci_cd_devops.container_orchestration: Azure managed containers (specific orchestration not named)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management; CTO performs monthly patch management reviews; workstations scanned daily for patch compliance; Windows patch management system + automatic network device patching

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Monitoring tool (name not disclosed) with predefined threshold alerts; capacity management controls for system performance
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled at application and infrastructure level; log access restricted; logs retained per policy
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts for high and critical risk events; alerts tracked in change-management/ticketing application

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure — compute, database, networking, security (IDS/IPS), key management (Key Vault), API Gateway
LLM inference for AI firmware generation agent (external API)
SSO / identity provider for user authentication

security_tools.antivirus: Endpoint Security Solution on all desktops/laptops and production servers; continuous auto-scan; anti-virus definitions updated daily from vendor; anti-malware and Trojan protection; email scanning
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scanning; self-assessment scan prior to every production release

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — physical access delegated to Azure (subservice); Azure responsible for data center security
A1.2 — environmental protections delegated to Azure and building management
Processing Integrity criteria — not relevant to system
Privacy criteria — not relevant to system
notable_controls: WorkOS SSO integration for all user authentication
Anthropic LLM API explicitly named in network diagram as external AI provider
Azure Key Vault for encryption key management
BCDR tested annually across multiple loss scenarios
Multi-AZ DR by spinning up servers across all Azure AZs
Vendor management includes quarterly risk assessment for non-SOC vendors

infrastructure.rationale: Azure VPC with multi-AZ, Azure API Gateway, Azure Key Vault, IaC baseline configs, segmented VPCs. Single region, no CDN, no WAF explicitly named — limits score.

application_architecture.rationale: Microservices on Azure with branch protection and required approvals. No named languages, frameworks, or compute type. WorkOS SSO integration is a maturity signal. Database type undisclosed.

data_layer.rationale: Azure managed database (type not named), Azure Key Vault encryption, multi-AZ. No caching layer, no file storage named, no data warehouse, backup frequency unquantified. High opacity.

security_posture.rationale: IDS/IPS via Azure, monthly vuln scans + pre-release self-assessment, annual external pen test, antivirus (daily definitions), MFA for admin access, TLS 1.3, Azure Key Vault, content filtering, CISO role defined. No WAF named.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC with rollback, branch protection with required approvals, separate environments, emergency change process, monthly patch reviews, daily compliance scans. CI/CD tool unnamed, compute type unnamed.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy, annual review and testing confirmed, multi-AZ failover via Azure AZ spinup. No stated RTO/RPO, no multi-region DR, backup frequency unquantified.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Azure is dominant single infrastructure provider. Anthropic is a critical LLM dependency for core product function. WorkOS is the SSO/auth provider. Three named vendors but two are critical single-vendor dependencies.

overall.rationale: SOC 2 Type 1 with zero exceptions. Notable for Anthropic LLM dependency made explicit in network diagram, WorkOS SSO, TLS 1.3, and Azure Key Vault. Significant gaps: 2-person team (CEO + CTO only), no WAF, database type undisclosed, no RTO/RPO, no cybersecurity insurance, Azure and Anthropic concentration risk.

red_flags: 2-person team (CEO + CTO only) — extreme key person risk for all security and engineering functions
No WAF explicitly named — application layer attack protection gap
No RTO/RPO disclosed — availability commitments unquantified
Dual critical dependency on Azure (infrastructure) AND Anthropic (LLM) — two existential single-vendor risks
Database type not named — complete opacity on primary data store
yellow_flags: CI/CD tool and compute type not named
No multi-region failover — DR limited to multi-AZ within single Azure region
Security criteria only — Availability, Confidentiality, Privacy not audited
Anthropic dependency creates AI model provider risk (rate limits, terms changes, model deprecation)
green_flags: TLS 1.3 explicitly specified — stronger than the generic 'TLS' standard in most peer reports
WorkOS SSO integration — enterprise-grade identity provider
Anthropic LLM dependency transparently disclosed in network diagram
Azure Key Vault for encryption key management
Azure API Gateway for controlled ingress
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning + pre-release self-assessment
Quarterly access reviews with 1-business-day revocation SLA
MFA enforced for all admin access, SCM access, and email access
BCDR tested annually with multiple loss scenarios
Unqualified audit opinion with zero exceptions
key_observations: BootLoop is unique in this batch for building an AI agent that interacts with physical hardware (oscilloscopes, debuggers) — the system boundary includes both cloud software AND physical test equipment, which is atypical for a cloud-hosted SaaS
Anthropic explicitly named as external AI provider in the network diagram (rare for ACCORP reports) — creates a direct dependency on Anthropic's API availability and terms of service
WorkOS for SSO shows deliberate use of a dedicated identity provider rather than rolling custom auth
Only 2 named roles (CEO + CTO) in the org chart — most extreme key-person concentration in this batch
TLS 1.3 specifically noted — most peers say 'TLS' generically; BootLoop specifying 1.3 suggests deliberate protocol choice
No database type or file storage named — embedded/firmware data (datasheets, schematics, hardware configs) stored in unspecified Azure managed service
Contact email (chris@bootloop.ai) differs from CEO name (Noah Pacik-Nelson) — suggests Chris is a technical/operational contact
